title: adr-kit project policy (.adr-kit.json)
description: Schema for the project-level policy file at docs/adr/.adr-kit.json (introduced in adr-kit v0.9.0). Field semantics are documented in the README Configuration section and the lint skill body. Top-level keys starting with '_' (e.g. '_comment', '_note') are silently allowed for embedding annotations and are not subject to validation.

strict_from.description: First ADR identifier (inclusive) on which all gates are enforced strictly. ADRs with a numerically lower number are linted in advisory mode for the gates that opt in via 'severity'. Format must match the canonical ADR-NNN pattern with three-digit zero-padded number.

ignore.description: List of ADR identifiers (ADR-NNN) or full filenames to skip entirely. Skipped ADRs appear in the aggregate as SKIPPED but produce no per-gate output.

severity.description: Per-gate severity. Default for completeness, evidence, clarity is 'advisory_before_strict_from' when strict_from is set, 'always_strict' otherwise. Default for consistency is 'always_strict' regardless: filename / heading mismatches and duplicate numbers are real bugs at any age.

template.description: Override the canonical seven required sections with a project-specific list. Useful for projects whose template legitimately differs from the toolkit's default.

required_sections.description: Required top-level headings, in the order they must appear. Each entry is the exact heading text including the '## ' prefix.

judge.description: Per-project policy for bin/adr-judge (introduced in adr-kit v0.12.0). Controls how Enforcement blocks are evaluated against a staged git diff at commit time. v0.13.0 added the llm_* knobs for the Sonnet-by-default LLM pass.

skip_files.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude from judging. Matches relative to the repo root. Common entries: 'tests/**', 'docs/**', 'vendor/**'. ADR-level path_globs still take precedence; this is a project-wide pre-filter.

advisory_only.description: When true, all violations from declarative rules are downgraded to advisory (the hook prints them but exits 0). Intended for early adoption phases where teams want visibility without blocking commits. Default false.

max_diff_bytes.description: Diffs larger than this size (in bytes) are skipped with a warning. Protects against pathological commits (e.g. generated-file dumps). Default 1048576 (1 MiB). Set 0 to disable the cap.

llm_default.description: When true, bin/adr-judge runs the LLM pass even without --llm on the command line. Useful when invoking the runner outside the pre-commit hook (e.g. CI). The hook template already passes --llm so this is independent of hook behaviour. Default false.

llm_model.description: Model identifier passed to `claude -p --model`. Default 'claude-sonnet-4-6'. Switch to 'claude-haiku-4-5' for higher commit throughput at lower cost, or to a current Sonnet/Opus tag if a newer release is preferred. Ignored when llm_cmd is set.

llm_cmd.description: Override the entire LLM invocation. Accepts either a tokenised array (e.g. ['claude','-p','--model','...']) or a single shell-quoted string that the runner shlex-splits. Useful for non-Claude-Code installs (e.g. an Anthropic SDK wrapper script) or for tests.

llm_timeout_seconds.description: Per-call timeout for the LLM pass in seconds. Default 120. Increase if your ADR set is large enough that one batch call routinely exceeds this.
